assumptions.statement: The only subject interpreted is the exact registered FMO-0.1 raw-byte artifact identified by the declared SHA-256 digest.
assumptions.check: The execution guard recomputes the subject digest and rejects any mismatch.

assumptions.statement: Each kernel variable has exactly one value in {false, true}, and not, and, or, implication, and biconditional use classical truth conditions.
assumptions.check: The analyzer exhaustively enumerates the declared two-valued domain and implements only the frozen expression operators.

assumptions.statement: Each module is checked independently; no result asserts that independently satisfying module models combine into one full FMO structure.
assumptions.check: The analyzer never constructs a cross-module Cartesian product, and every output retains its module identifier.

assumptions.statement: The mapping from FMO-0.1 natural-language statements to Boolean variables and constraints is an analyst interpretation, not a reproduced fact or accepted equivalence.
assumptions.check: ANALYSIS-FMO-07-SEMANTIC-VALIDITY requires independent line-by-line human adjudication and can invalidate machine analyses.

assumptions.statement: Exhaustiveness means all Boolean valuations inside each declared finite module only; it does not mean exhaustive histories, actors, objects, policies, times, boundaries, or external systems.
assumptions.check: The formal-system artifact records module sizes and explicitly denies cross-module or unbounded coverage.

assumptions.statement: An intended entailment holds within the kernel only when no satisfying valuation in its named module makes its premise true and conclusion false.
assumptions.check: The analyzer retains a violating valuation when found and otherwise reports only holds_within_kernel.

assumptions.statement: A non-entailment is witnessed within the kernel only by a retained satisfying valuation whose premise is true and conclusion false.
assumptions.check: The analyzer records the complete module-local witness assignment or records that no witness was found.

assumptions.statement: An invariant is independent within one kernel module only when removing its constraint permits a valuation that satisfies every remaining module constraint and violates the removed constraint.
assumptions.check: The analyzer performs one omission search per invariant and retains the complete witness if one exists.

assumptions.statement: PERMIT, DENY, and UNRESOLVED are opaque Boolean observations in this kernel; no policy-composition, legitimacy, jurisdiction, delegation, revocation, or liveness semantics is supplied.
assumptions.check: The authority analysis is restricted to the declared state-change guard and explicitly leaves FMO-C02 unresolved outside the kernel.

assumptions.statement: Deletion variables describe a frozen declared scope; closure completeness, materialization, reconstruction, exceptions, and verifier correctness are not independently established by the kernel.
assumptions.check: The deletion analysis treats unknown, accessible, reconstructable, or local-erasure-only cases as boundary inputs and does not infer physical or external erasure.

assumptions.statement: The planned analyzer uses CPython 3.12.13 standard library only, makes no network or model call, consumes no random input, and writes a new output path without overwriting it.
assumptions.check: The execution configuration, tool-provenance artifact, script guard, and retained input digests are compared at freeze and execution.

assumptions.statement: No planned analyzer output or hand-adjudicated result may be created or inspected before an authoritative FROZEN manifest and freeze attestation exist.
assumptions.check: The analyzer rejects a DRAFT manifest, the draft package contains no result artifact, and freeze review must attest no prior result access.
